h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: The ticks show the checks were done in sequence: identity, allergies, observations read back, escalation box, correct item.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: 'Correct item' was me letting the tick do the judgement work.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior learner turns were moving toward correctly labelling the unsafe inference as a formula while still treating the checklist sequence as a neutral, completed task — the expected next step was simply to fill in the handover template.

scored.recoheredEarlier: the price tag still looks like a big accusation in the window until that LIMITED BREAD notice and the invoice shove into the picture
scored.statedInsightEvidence: My old line was treating the 35p like one big guilty headline, when the notice is saying, 'No, split the headline: cost went up, boxes got cut, then there's whatever extra the shop chose.'
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward a continued, if qualified, suspicion of the shopkeeper, with the learner progressively accepting cost-and-shortage as partial excuses while still treating the price card as the primary accusation.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Across doubled, so I was calling the board double. That still looks obvious until the chalk makes it two down as well. Fine: four tiles, not two. Is the mistake that I counted only the width?
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Bad wording, fine, but that was the dodge.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a trajectory of accepting 'bad wording' as the full explanation of the mistake and consolidating the correct count.
